How do we create ad campaigns that are able to bring people into our funnel? And then how do we funnel them in to convert as well? The four parts of the Advertising Core 4 are product, audience, offer and funnel. The product is, do you have a solution or opportunity? At the simplest level, if you can't answer this with the product that you've got, then you're in trouble. A solution is either it's a known problem or you're creating a problem in the market and making people aware, or an opportunity could be something which is going to benefit them, improve their life, etc. Either way, your product has to demonstrate that it can do something for that user. Then the audience is identifying your ideal customer. And it's going beyond what age they are and gender and things like that. It's going a bit deeper into who's your perfect customer. Once you break down your perfect customer, then you can think about different versions of that perfect customer.
